records.question: Which users named Helen have reviewed the item titled avocado?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT useracct.u_id FROM useracct
JOIN review ON useracct.u_id = review.u_id
JOIN item ON review.i_id = item.i_id
WHERE item.title = 'avocado' AND useracct.name = 'Helen'
records.pred_sql: select useracct.u_id from useracct join review on useracct.u_id = review.u_id join item on review.i_id = item.i_id where useracct.name like '%helen%' and item.title like '%avocado%' limit 5;
records.thought: To find users named Helen who have reviewed the item titled avocado, I need to join the useracct and review tables based on the user ID, and also join with the item table to filter by the title.

records.question: Which users named Helen have reviews with a rank exactly equal to 3?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT useracct.u_id FROM useracct
JOIN review ON useracct.u_id = review.u_id
WHERE review.rank = 3 AND useracct.name = 'Helen'
records.pred_sql: select useracct.u_id from useracct join review on useracct.u_id = review.u_id where useracct.name like '%helen%' and review.rank = 3 limit 5;
records.thought: To find users named Helen with reviews that have a rank of exactly 3, I will join the useracct and review tables on the user ID and filter by the name and rank criteria.

records.question: Which user accounts have written reviews with a rank of at least 1?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT useracct.u_id FROM useracct
JOIN review ON useracct.u_id = review.u_id
JOIN item ON review.i_id = item.i_id
WHERE review.rank >= 1
records.pred_sql: select useracct.u_id from useracct join review on useracct.u_id = review.u_id where review.rank >= 1 limit 5;
records.thought: To find user accounts that have written reviews with a rank of at least 1, I will join the useracct and review tables on the user ID and filter the reviews based on the rank condition.

records.question: Which users named Helen gave a 5-star review to the shampoo product?

SELECT useracct.u_id FROM useracct
JOIN review ON useracct.u_id = review.u_id
JOIN item ON review.i_id = item.i_id
WHERE item.title = 'shampoo' AND review.rating = 5 AND useracct.name = 'Helen'
records.pred_sql: select useracct.u_id from useracct join review on useracct.u_id = review.u_id join item on review.i_id = item.i_id where useracct.name like '%helen%' and review.rating = 5 and item.title like '%shampoo%' limit 5;

records.question: Who in the user accounts has the name Mark?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT useracct.u_id FROM useracct
WHERE useracct.name = 'Mark'
records.pred_sql: select useracct.u_id from useracct where name like '%mark%' limit 5;
records.thought: The query needs to find user accounts with the name 'Mark', so we will filter the useracct table based on the name column using a LIKE clause.

records.question: Which users named Helen have reviews with a rating exactly 10 and a rank of at least 1?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT useracct.u_id FROM useracct
JOIN review ON useracct.u_id = review.u_id
WHERE review.rank >= 1 AND useracct.name = 'Helen' AND review.rating = 10
records.pred_sql: select useracct.u_id from useracct join review on useracct.u_id = review.u_id where useracct.name like '%helen%' and review.rating = 10 and review.rank >= 1 limit 5;
records.thought: To find users named Helen with specific review criteria, I will join the useracct and review tables on the user ID, and then filter by the name, rating, and rank conditions.

records.question: Which user accounts belong to Mark and have reviews with a rank of at most 7?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT useracct.u_id FROM useracct
JOIN review ON useracct.u_id = review.u_id
WHERE useracct.name = 'Mark' AND review.rank <= 7
records.pred_sql: select useracct.u_id from useracct join review on useracct.u_id = review.u_id where useracct.name like '%mark%' and review.rank <= 7 limit 5;
records.thought: To find user accounts belonging to Mark with reviews of rank at most 7, I will join the useracct and review tables on the user ID and filter by the name 'mark' and rank <= 7.

records.question: What user accounts named Helen have reviews with a rating of at most 5 and a rank of at least 5?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT useracct.u_id FROM useracct
JOIN review ON useracct.u_id = review.u_id
WHERE review.rating <= 5 AND review.rank >= 5 AND useracct.name = 'Helen'
records.pred_sql: select useracct.u_id from useracct join review on useracct.u_id = review.u_id where useracct.name like '%helen%' and review.rating <= 5 and review.rank >= 5 limit 5;
records.thought: The query needs to find user accounts named 'Helen' who have reviews with specific rating and rank criteria. This involves joining the useracct and review tables based on the user ID.

records.question: Which users named Mark have given a rank 2 review with a rating of at most 5 for the item titled orange?
records.gold_sql:
SELECT useracct.u_id FROM useracct
JOIN review ON useracct.u_id = review.u_id
JOIN item ON review.i_id = item.i_id
WHERE useracct.name = 'Mark' AND review.rank = 2 AND item.title = 'orange' AND review.rating <= 5
records.pred_sql: select useracct.u_id from useracct join review on useracct.u_id = review.u_id join item on review.i_id = item.i_id where useracct.name like '%mark%' and review.rank = 2 and review.rating <= 5 and item.title like '%orange%' limit 5;
records.thought: To find users named Mark who have given a rank 2 review with a rating of at most 5 for the item titled 'orange', I will join the useracct, review, and item tables based on their relationships and apply the necessary filters.
